response.justification: Bogdan Szabo is a seasoned Staff-level engineer with 16+ years of experience across web, embedded, and data systems, currently at Google contributing to MCP frameworks and agentic workflow tooling. However, the role requires 8+ years of AI-driven solution development — the candidate's GenAI exposure appears limited to roughly one year at Google. Python, the dominant language for production GenAI work, is entirely absent from the listed skills. There is no demonstrated experience with vector databases, retrieval-augmented generation architectures, LLM evaluation pipelines, or multi-agent orchestration frameworks such as LangGraph or CrewAI, all of which are central to this position.
strengths: Staff-level engineering pedigree at top-tier companies (Google, Meta) with demonstrated TypeScript expertise across production systems
Direct current involvement in Model Context Protocol (MCP) code generation and agentic workflow infrastructure at Google
Broad polyglot background across cloud (AWS CDK), distributed systems, and data pipelines demonstrates adaptability
concerns: Python is entirely absent from the skills section despite being the primary language for GenAI and data-pipeline work in this role
No evidence of experience with vector databases, RAG architectures, LLM evaluation, or named multi-agent frameworks
AI/GenAI experience is at most ~1 year (current Google role), far below the 8-year minimum requirement

key_factors.factor: MCP and agentic tooling at Google
